---
title: "Rmd_Main"
author: "Diederik"
date: "2026-02-25"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r cars}
remotes::install_github('jaburgoyne/compmus')
```
```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(compmus)
```


```{r}
bzt <- read_csv("~/Desktop/Linguistics/Year 4/Semester 2/Computational Musicology/CSV files/bzt-pitches.csv")
bzt <- read_csv("~/Desktop/Linguistics/Year 4/Semester 2/Computational Musicology/CSV files/bzt-timbre.csv")
bzt |>
  compmus_wrangle_timbre() |> 
  mutate(timbre = map(timbre, compmus_normalise, "manhattan")) |>
  compmus_gather_timbre() |>
  ggplot(
    aes(
      x = start + duration / 2,
      width = duration,
      y = mfcc,
      fill = value
    )
  ) +
  geom_tile() +
  labs(x = "Time (s)", y = NULL, fill = "Magnitude") +
  scale_fill_viridis_c() +                              
  theme_classic()



```
